Course Title: AI in Exams and Assessments: Fairness and Integrity
Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Teachers, Professors, Academic administrators, Exam coordinators, Assessment specialists, Education technology coordinators, Student affairs staff, Academic integrity officers
Team or Dept this is for: Education, Academic Affairs, Student Services, IT, Compliance, Quality Assurance
Description: Detection errors, false accusations, and system manipulation.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with how exams or assessments are conducted in their organisation or institution, along with a general awareness that AI tools exist and are increasingly used in educational settings.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key limitations of AI detection tools in educational assessments and explain how false accusations can arise from detection errors. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to recognise and respond to attempts to manipulate AI-based assessment systems, helping to protect fairness and integrity in your organisation.
